metadata.description: Tool dependency graph for Google Super and GitHub (Composio toolkits). Edges show parameter flows between tools (source=tool) or from the user/agent (source=user). An agent uses this graph to decide: run a prerequisite tool OR prompt the user.

nodes.label: User / Agent
nodes.title:
User / Agent
Parameters supplied directly by the user or inferred from conversation context.

nodes.label: List Threads
nodes.title:
List Threads
Retrieve all Gmail conversation threads. Returns thread_id list.
Provides: thread_id

nodes.label: Get Thread
nodes.title:
Get Thread
Fetch full details of a single Gmail thread. Requires thread_id.
Requires (tool): thread_id

nodes.label: Reply to Thread
nodes.title:
Reply to Thread
Send a reply within an existing Gmail thread. Requires thread_id.
Requires (tool): thread_id
Requires (user): body

nodes.label: List Messages
nodes.title:
List Messages
List Gmail messages (with optional query filters). Returns message_ids.
Provides: message_id

nodes.label: Get Message
nodes.title:
Get Message
Fetch full content of a specific Gmail message. Requires message_id.
Requires (tool): message_id

nodes.label: Send Email
nodes.title:
Send Email
Compose and send a new Gmail message. Requires recipient email address.
Requires (tool): email
Requires (user): subject, body

user_inputs.description: Recipient email (alternative: derive via Search Contacts)

nodes.label: Delete Message
nodes.title:
Delete Message
Permanently delete a Gmail message. Requires message_id.
Requires (tool): message_id

nodes.label: List Drafts
nodes.title:
List Drafts
List all Gmail drafts. Returns draft_ids.
Provides: draft_id

nodes.label: Create Draft
nodes.title:
Create Draft
Create a new Gmail draft message. Returns draft_id.
Provides: draft_id

nodes.label: Get Draft
nodes.title:
Get Draft
Retrieve content of a specific Gmail draft. Requires draft_id.
Requires (tool): draft_id

nodes.label: Send Draft
nodes.title:
Send Draft
Send an existing Gmail draft. Requires draft_id.
Requires (tool): draft_id

nodes.label: List Labels
nodes.title:
List Labels
Retrieve all Gmail labels. Returns label_ids and names.
Provides: label_id

nodes.label: Create Label
nodes.title:
Create Label
Create a new Gmail label. Returns label_id.
Provides: label_id
Requires (user): name

nodes.label: Modify Email Labels
nodes.title:
Modify Email Labels
Adds and/or removes specified Gmail labels for a message; ensure `message_id` and all `label_ids` are valid (use 'listLabels' for custom label IDs).
Requires (tool): message_id, label_id

nodes.label: Batch Delete Messages
nodes.title:
Batch Delete Messages
Tool to permanently delete multiple Gmail messages in bulk, bypassing Trash with no recovery possible. Use when you need to efficiently remove large numbers of emails (e.g., retention enforcement, mailbox hygiene). Use GMAIL_MOVE_TO_TRASH instead when reversibility may be needed. Always obtain explicit user confirmation and verify a sample of message IDs before executing. High-volume calls may trigger 429 rateLimitExceeded or 403 userRateLimitExceeded errors; apply exponential backoff.
Requires (tool): message_id

nodes.label: Batch Modify Messages
nodes.title:
Batch Modify Messages
Modify labels on multiple Gmail messages in one efficient API call. Supports up to 1,000 messages per request for bulk operations like archiving, marking as read/unread, or applying custom labels. High-volume calls may return 429 rateLimitExceeded or 403 userRateLimitExceeded; apply exponential backoff.
Requires (tool): message_id, label_id

nodes.label: List Contacts
nodes.title:
List Contacts
List all Google Contacts. Returns contact names and email addresses.
Provides: email, contact_id

nodes.label: Search Contacts
nodes.title:
Search Contacts
Search contacts by name or email. Returns matching email addresses. Used to look up email from a person's name.
Provides: email, contact_id
Requires (user): query

user_inputs.description: Person's name or partial email to search for

nodes.label: List Calendars
nodes.title:
List Calendars
List all calendars on the user's calendar list. Returns calendar_ids.
Provides: calendar_id

nodes.label: Create Calendar
nodes.title:
Create Calendar
Create a new secondary Google Calendar. Returns calendar_id.
Provides: calendar_id
Requires (user): summary

nodes.label: Get Calendar
nodes.title:
Get Calendar
Retrieves metadata for a SINGLE specific calendar from the user's calendar list by its calendar ID. This action requires a calendarId parameter and returns details about that one calendar only. NOTE: This does NOT list all calendars. To list all calendars in the user's calendar list, use GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDAR_LIST_LIST instead.
Requires (tool): calendar_id

nodes.label: Update Calendar
nodes.title:
Update Calendar
Full PUT-style update that overwrites all calendar metadata fields; unspecified optional fields are cleared. Use GOOGLECALENDAR_PATCH_CALENDAR to update only a subset of fields. Mutates the underlying calendar resource (title, description, timeZone, etc.); use GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDAR_LIST_UPDATE to change per-user display properties like color.
Requires (tool): calendar_id

nodes.label: Delete Calendar
nodes.title:
Delete Calendar
Deletes a secondary calendar that you own or have delete permissions on. Deletion is permanent and irreversible — verify the correct calendar_id before calling. You cannot delete your primary calendar or calendars you only have read/write access to. Use calendarList.list to find calendars with owner accessRole. For primary calendars, use calendars.clear instead. Parallel calls may trigger userRateLimitExceeded; sequence bulk deletions.
Requires (tool): calendar_id

nodes.label: List Events
nodes.title:
List Events
Returns events on the specified calendar. TIMEZONE WARNING: When using timeMin/timeMax with UTC timestamps (ending in 'Z'), the time window is interpreted in UTC regardless of the calendar's timezone. For example, querying '2026-01-19T00:00:00Z' to '2026-01-20T00:00:00Z' on a calendar in America/Los_Angeles (UTC-8) covers 2026-01-18 4pm to 2026-01-19 4pm local time, potentially missing events on the intended local date. To query for a specific local date, use timestamps with the appropriate timezone offset in timeMin/timeMax (e.g., '2026-01-19T00:00:00-08:00' for PST).
Provides: event_id
Requires (tool): calendar_id

nodes.label: Get Event
nodes.title:
Get Event
Retrieves a SINGLE event by its unique event_id (REQUIRED). This action does NOT list or search events - it fetches ONE specific event when you already know its ID. If you want to list events within a time range, search for events, or filter by criteria like time_min/time_max, use GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST instead.
Requires (tool): calendar_id, event_id

nodes.label: Create Event
nodes.title:
Create Event
Create a new event in a calendar. Requires calendar_id.
Provides: event_id
Requires (tool): calendar_id
Requires (user): summary, start_time, end_time

nodes.label: Update Event
nodes.title:
Update Event
Update an existing calendar event. Requires calendar_id + event_id.
Requires (tool): calendar_id, event_id

nodes.label: Delete Event
nodes.title:
Delete Event
Delete a calendar event. Requires calendar_id + event_id.
Requires (tool): calendar_id, event_id

nodes.label: Clear Calendar
nodes.title:
Clear Calendar
Clears a primary calendar by deleting all events from it. The calendar itself is preserved; only its events are removed. Primary calendars cannot be deleted entirely.
Requires (tool): calendar_id

nodes.label: List ACL Rules
nodes.title:
List ACL Rules
Retrieves the list of access control rules (ACLs) for a specified calendar, providing the necessary 'rule_id' values required for updating specific ACL rules.
Provides: rule_id
Requires (tool): calendar_id

nodes.label: Create ACL Rule
nodes.title:
Create ACL Rule
Creates an access control rule for a calendar. Use when you need to grant sharing permissions to a user, group, or domain.
Provides: rule_id
Requires (tool): calendar_id
Requires (user): role, scope_type, scope_value

user_inputs.description: Email address or domain to grant access

nodes.label: Delete ACL Rule
nodes.title:
Delete ACL Rule
Deletes an access control rule from a Google Calendar. Use when you need to remove sharing permissions for a user, group, or domain.
Requires (tool): calendar_id, rule_id

nodes.label: Update ACL Rule
nodes.title:
Update ACL Rule
Updates an access control rule for the specified calendar.
Requires (tool): calendar_id, rule_id

nodes.label: List Files
nodes.title:
List Files
List files in Google Drive. Returns file_ids and metadata.
Provides: file_id

nodes.label: Search Files
nodes.title:
Search Files
Search Drive files using query syntax. Returns matching file_ids.
Provides: file_id

nodes.label: Create File
nodes.title:
Create File
Create a new file in Google Drive. Returns file_id.
Provides: file_id
Requires (user): name

user_inputs.description: File content or URL to upload

nodes.label: Get File Metadata
nodes.title:
Get File Metadata
Retrieve metadata for a specific Drive file. Requires file_id.
Requires (tool): file_id

nodes.label: Delete File
nodes.title:
Delete File
Permanently delete a Drive file. Requires file_id.
Requires (tool): file_id

nodes.label: Download File
nodes.title:
Download File
Download file content from Drive. Requires file_id.
Requires (tool): file_id

nodes.label: Copy File
nodes.title:
Copy File
Creates a copy of a file and applies any requested updates with patch semantics. Use when you need to duplicate a file with advanced options like label inclusion, visibility settings, or custom metadata.
Provides: file_id
Requires (tool): file_id

nodes.label: Comment on File
nodes.title:
Comment on File
Tool to create a comment on a file in Google Drive. Returns a nested `data` object; extract `data.id` for the resulting comment identifier. Omit `anchor` and `quoted_file_content_*` for general file-level comments.
Requires (tool): file_id
Requires (user): content

nodes.label: Share File
nodes.title:
Share File
DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLEDRIVE_CREATE_PERMISSION instead; use GOOGLEDRIVE_UPDATE_PERMISSION to modify existing permissions (avoids duplicate entries). Modifies sharing permissions for an existing Google Drive file, granting a specified role to a user, group, domain, or 'anyone'. Bulk calls may trigger 403 rateLimitExceeded (~100 req/100s/user); use jittered exponential backoff.
Requires (tool): file_id
Requires (user): role, type

user_inputs.description: Permission role: reader | writer | commenter | owner
Principal type: user | group | domain | anyone
Email of user/group to share with

nodes.label: List Folders
nodes.title:
List Folders
List folders in Google Drive. Returns folder_ids.
Provides: folder_id

nodes.label: Move to Folder
nodes.title:
Move to Folder
Tool to add a parent folder for a file using Google Drive API v2. Use when you need to add a file to an additional folder.
Requires (tool): file_id, folder_id

nodes.label: List Spreadsheets
nodes.title:
List Spreadsheets
List all Google Sheets spreadsheets in Drive. Returns spreadsheet_ids.
Provides: spreadsheet_id

nodes.label: Create Spreadsheet
nodes.title:
Create Spreadsheet
Create a new Google Sheets spreadsheet. Returns spreadsheet_id.
Provides: spreadsheet_id
Requires (user): title

nodes.label: Read Cell Ranges
nodes.title:
Read Cell Ranges
Retrieves data from specified cell ranges in a Google Spreadsheet.
Requires (tool): spreadsheet_id

nodes.label: Add Sheet Tab
nodes.title:
Add Sheet Tab
Adds a new sheet to a spreadsheet. Supports three sheet types: GRID, OBJECT, and DATA_SOURCE. SHEET TYPES: - GRID (default): Standard spreadsheet with rows/columns. Use properties to set dimensions, tab color, etc. - OBJECT: Sheet containing a chart. Requires objectSheetConfig with chartSpec (basicChart or pieChart). - DATA_SOURCE: Sheet connected to BigQuery. Requires dataSourceConfig with bigQuery spec and bigquery.readonly OAuth scope. OTHER NOTES: - Sheet names must be unique; use forceUnique=true to auto-append suffix (_2, _3) if name exists - For tab colors, use EITHER rgbColor OR themeColor, not both - Avoid 'index' when creating sheets in parallel (causes errors) - OBJECT sheets are created via addChart with position.newSheet=true - DATA_SOURCE sheets require bigquery.readonly OAuth scope Use cases: Add standard grid sheet, create chart on dedicated sheet, connect to BigQuery data source.
Provides: sheet_id
Requires (tool): spreadsheet_id

nodes.label: Append Rows/Columns
nodes.title:
Append Rows/Columns
Tool to append new rows or columns to a sheet, increasing its size. Use when you need to add empty rows or columns to an existing sheet.
Requires (tool): spreadsheet_id, sheet_id

nodes.label: Create Chart
nodes.title:
Create Chart
Create a chart in a Google Sheets spreadsheet using the specified data range and chart type. Conditional requirements: - Provide either a simple chart via chart_type + data_range (basicChart), OR supply a full chart_spec supporting all chart types. Exactly one approach should be used. - When using chart_spec, set exactly one of the union fields (basicChart | pieChart | bubbleChart | candlestickChart | histogramChart | waterfallChart | treemapChart | orgChart | scorecardChart).
Requires (tool): spreadsheet_id, sheet_id

nodes.label: Clear Cells
nodes.title:
Clear Cells
Clears cell content (preserving formatting and notes) from a specified A1 notation range in a Google Spreadsheet; the range must correspond to an existing sheet and cells.
Requires (tool): spreadsheet_id
Requires (user): range

nodes.label: Write Cells
nodes.title:
Write Cells
DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_VALUES_UPDATE instead. Write values to ONE range in a Google Sheet, or append as new rows if no start cell is given. IMPORTANT - This tool does NOT accept the Google Sheets API's native batch format: - WRONG: {"data": [{"range": "...", "values": [[...]]}], ...} - CORRECT: {"sheet_name": "...", "values": [[...]], "first_cell_location": "...", ...} To update MULTIPLE ranges, make SEPARATE CALLS to this tool for each range. Features: - Auto-expands grid for large datasets (prevents range errors) - Set first_cell_location to write at a specific position (e.g., "A1", "B5") - Omit first_cell_location to append values as new rows at the end Requirements: Target sheet must exist and spreadsheet must contain at least one worksheet.
Requires (tool): spreadsheet_id
Requires (user): values, range

user_inputs.description: 2D array of cell values to write

nodes.label: Aggregate Column
nodes.title:
Aggregate Column
Searches for rows where a specific column matches a value and performs mathematical operations on data from another column.
Requires (tool): spreadsheet_id
Requires (user): operation, target_column, sheet_name

user_inputs.description: Operation: sum | average | count | min | max

nodes.label: Auto-Resize Columns
nodes.title:
Auto-Resize Columns
Auto-fit column widths or row heights for a dimension range using batchUpdate.autoResizeDimensions. Use when you need to automatically adjust row heights or column widths to fit content after writing data.
Requires (tool): spreadsheet_id

nodes.label: List Task Lists
nodes.title:
List Task Lists
List all Google Task Lists. Returns tasklist_ids.
Provides: tasklist_id

nodes.label: Create Task List
nodes.title:
Create Task List
Create a new Google Task List. Returns tasklist_id.
Provides: tasklist_id
Requires (user): title

nodes.label: List Tasks
nodes.title:
List Tasks
List tasks in a task list. Requires tasklist_id. Returns task_ids.
Provides: task_id
Requires (tool): tasklist_id

nodes.label: Get Task
nodes.title:
Get Task
Retrieve details of a specific task. Requires tasklist_id + task_id.
Requires (tool): tasklist_id, task_id

nodes.label: Create Task
nodes.title:
Create Task
Create a new task in a task list. Requires tasklist_id.
Provides: task_id
Requires (tool): tasklist_id
Requires (user): title

nodes.label: Update Task
nodes.title:
Update Task
Update an existing task. Requires tasklist_id + task_id.
Requires (tool): tasklist_id, task_id

nodes.label: Delete Task
nodes.title:
Delete Task
Delete a task from a list. Requires tasklist_id + task_id.
Requires (tool): tasklist_id, task_id

nodes.label: Clear Completed Tasks
nodes.title:
Clear Completed Tasks
Permanently and irreversibly clears all completed tasks from a specified Google Tasks list; this action is destructive, idempotent, and cannot be undone. Always require explicit user confirmation before invoking.
Requires (tool): tasklist_id

nodes.label: Bulk Create Tasks
nodes.title:
Bulk Create Tasks
DEPRECATED: Use BatchExecute instead. Creates multiple tasks in a Google Tasks list in a single operation using HTTP batching. Use when you need to create many tasks efficiently (reducing round-trips compared to individual insert calls).
Requires (tool): tasklist_id
Requires (user): tasks

user_inputs.description: Array of task objects [{title, notes?, due?}]

nodes.label: List Albums
nodes.title:
List Albums
List all Google Photos albums. Returns album_ids.
Provides: album_id

nodes.label: Create Album
nodes.title:
Create Album
Creates a new album in Google Photos.
Provides: album_id
Requires (user): title

nodes.label: List Media Items
nodes.title:
List Media Items
List photos/videos from Google Photos. Returns media_item_ids.
Provides: media_item_id

nodes.label: Upload Media
nodes.title:
Upload Media
Batch upload and create media items in Google Photos. Supports three input methods: 1. 'urls': Simple list of public URLs (file names extracted automatically) 2. 'media_files': List of objects with url/file, file_name, and description 3. 'files': List of FileUploadable objects for pre-uploaded files Media items can optionally be added to an album at a specific position. Maximum 50 items per request.
Provides: media_item_id

nodes.label: Add Media to Album
nodes.title:
Add Media to Album
Adds one or more media items to an album in Google Photos.
Requires (tool): album_id, media_item_id

nodes.label: Get Media Details
nodes.title:
Get Media Details
Returns the list of media items for the specified media item identifiers.
Requires (tool): media_item_id

nodes.label: Add Album Enrichment
nodes.title:
Add Album Enrichment
Adds an enrichment at a specified position in a defined album.
Requires (tool): album_id

nodes.label: List GA4 Properties
nodes.title:
List GA4 Properties
List Google Analytics 4 properties. Returns property IDs.
Provides: ga4_property

user_inputs.description: GA4 account ID (if filtering by account)

nodes.label: Run Analytics Reports
nodes.title:
Run Analytics Reports
Tool to return multiple analytics data reports in a batch. Use when you need to fetch multiple reports for one GA4 property in a single request.
Requires (tool): ga4_property
Requires (user): dateRanges, metrics

nodes.label: Run Pivot Reports
nodes.title:
Run Pivot Reports
Tool to return multiple pivot reports in a batch for a GA4 property. Use when you need to fetch multiple pivot table reports with multi-dimensional analysis in a single request.
Requires (tool): ga4_property
Requires (user): dateRanges, pivots, metrics

user_inputs.description: Date range(s) for the pivot report

nodes.label: Create Custom Dimension
nodes.title:
Create Custom Dimension
Tool to create a CustomDimension for a Google Analytics property. Use when you need to add a new custom dimension to track specific user properties, event parameters, or eCommerce item parameters.
Provides: dimension_name
Requires (tool): ga4_property
Requires (user): displayName, scope

nodes.label: Archive Custom Dimension
nodes.title:
Archive Custom Dimension
Tool to archive a CustomDimension on a property. Use when you need to remove a custom dimension from active use without permanently deleting it. Archived dimensions cannot be used in new reports.
Requires (tool): dimension_name

nodes.label: List My Repos
nodes.title:
List My Repos
List repositories for the authenticated user. Returns owner + repo names.
Provides: owner, repo

nodes.label: List Org Repos
nodes.title:
List Org Repos
List repositories in an organization. Requires org name. Returns owner + repo.
Provides: owner, repo
Requires (tool): org

nodes.label: Create Repo
nodes.title:
Create Repo
Create a new GitHub repository. Returns owner + repo.
Provides: owner, repo
Requires (user): name

nodes.label: Get Repo
nodes.title:
Get Repo
Get full repository metadata. Requires owner + repo.
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Delete Repo
nodes.title:
Delete Repo
Permanently delete a repository. Requires owner + repo.
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Fork Repo
nodes.title:
Fork Repo
Fork a repository to another account. Requires owner + repo.
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: List Branches
nodes.title:
List Branches
Lists branches for an existing GitHub repository, with an option to filter by protection status.
Provides: branch_name
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Create Branch
nodes.title:
Create Branch
Create a new branch from a commit SHA. Requires owner + repo + sha.
Provides: branch_name
Requires (tool): owner, repo, commit_sha

nodes.label: List Commits
nodes.title:
List Commits
Lists commits for a GitHub repository. Requires 'owner' (username/org) and 'repo' (repository name) parameters. Optionally filter by SHA/branch, path, author, committer, or date range.
Provides: commit_sha
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Get Commit
nodes.title:
Get Commit
Fetch full details of a commit. Requires owner + repo + commit_sha.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, commit_sha

nodes.label: Compare Commits
nodes.title:
Compare Commits
Compares two commit points (commits, branches, tags, or SHAs) within a repository or across forks, using `BASE...HEAD` or `OWNER:REF...OWNER:REF` format for the `basehead` parameter.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, commit_sha

nodes.label: Create Blob
nodes.title:
Create Blob
Creates a Git blob in a repository, requiring content and encoding ('utf-8' or 'base64'). Requires write access to the repository.
Provides: blob_sha
Requires (tool): owner, repo
Requires (user): content

user_inputs.description: File content to store as a Git blob

nodes.label: Create Tree
nodes.title:
Create Tree
Creates a new Git tree object in a repository, defining file/directory structure by specifying tree entries, optionally building on a `base_tree` SHA; all provided SHAs must be valid.
Provides: tree_sha
Requires (tool): owner, repo, blob_sha
Requires (user): tree

user_inputs.description: Tree entries [{path, mode, type, sha or content}]

nodes.label: Create Commit
nodes.title:
Create Commit
Creates a new commit in a GitHub repository; the `tree` SHA and any `parents` SHAs must already exist in the repository.
Provides: commit_sha
Requires (tool): owner, repo, tree_sha, commit_sha
Requires (user): message

user_inputs.example: fix: resolve null pointer in auth

nodes.label: Commit Multiple Files
nodes.title:
Commit Multiple Files
Tool to atomically create, update, or delete multiple files in a GitHub repository as a single commit. Uses Git Data APIs to avoid SHA mismatch conflicts that occur with the Contents API when multiple files are modified in parallel. Use when you need to make multi-file changes reliably. BRANCH CREATION: When committing to a new branch (e.g., 'fix/my-fix' or 'feature/new-feature'), you MUST provide 'base_branch' (typically 'main' or 'master') to create the branch from. If the branch already exists, base_branch is not needed. This action handles race conditions automatically: if the branch is updated by another commit between fetching the HEAD and updating the reference (resulting in a 422 'not a fast forward' error), the action will retry by refetching the HEAD and rebasing changes. Use max_retries to control this behavior.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, branch_name
Requires (user): message

nodes.label: Comment on Commit
nodes.title:
Comment on Commit
Creates a comment on a specific commit, or on a specific line if `path` and `position` are provided.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, commit_sha
Requires (user): body

nodes.label: Set Commit Status
nodes.title:
Set Commit Status
Sets a commit's status (e.g., error, failure, pending, success from CI/CD) for a given SHA; max 1000 statuses per SHA/context.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, commit_sha
Requires (user): state

user_inputs.description: CI status: error | failure | pending | success

nodes.label: List Issues
nodes.title:
List Issues
List issues for a repository. Requires owner + repo. Returns issue_numbers.
Provides: issue_number
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Get Issue
nodes.title:
Get Issue
Retrieve full details of an issue. Requires owner + repo + issue_number.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, issue_number

nodes.label: Create Issue
nodes.title:
Create Issue
Open a new issue in a repository. Requires owner + repo.
Provides: issue_number
Requires (tool): owner, repo
Requires (user): title

nodes.label: Update Issue
nodes.title:
Update Issue
Edit an existing issue (title, body, state, etc.). Requires owner + repo + issue_number.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, issue_number

nodes.label: Assign Users to Issue
nodes.title:
Assign Users to Issue
Adds assignees to a GitHub issue. This action only adds users - it does not remove existing assignees. Changes are silently ignored if the authenticated user lacks push access to the repository.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, issue_number

nodes.label: Add Labels to Issue
nodes.title:
Add Labels to Issue
Adds labels (provided in the request body) to a repository issue; labels that do not already exist are created.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, issue_number, label_name

nodes.label: List Repo Labels
nodes.title:
List Repo Labels
List all labels in a repository. Requires owner + repo. Returns label_names.
Provides: label_name
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Create Label
nodes.title:
Create Label
Create a new label in a repository. Requires owner + repo. Returns label_name.
Provides: label_name
Requires (tool): owner, repo
Requires (user): name, color

nodes.label: List Pull Requests
nodes.title:
List Pull Requests
Lists pull requests for a GitHub repository. REQUIRES 'owner' AND 'repo' parameters - both are MANDATORY and must be provided in every call. Does NOT support filtering by assignee or date range - use GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS instead for searching PRs by assignee, author, or across multiple repositories.
Provides: pull_number
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Get Pull Request
nodes.title:
Get Pull Request
Retrieve full PR details. Requires owner + repo + pull_number.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, pull_number

nodes.label: Create Pull Request
nodes.title:
Create Pull Request
Open a new pull request. Requires owner + repo + head branch + base branch.
Provides: pull_number
Requires (tool): owner, repo, branch_name
Requires (user): title, base

user_inputs.description: Target branch to merge into (e.g. main)

nodes.label: Merge Pull Request
nodes.title:
Merge Pull Request
Merge a pull request. Requires owner + repo + pull_number.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, pull_number

nodes.label: Check PR Merged
nodes.title:
Check PR Merged
Checks if a specified GitHub pull request has been merged, indicated by a 204 HTTP status (merged) or 404 (not merged/found).
Requires (tool): owner, repo, pull_number

nodes.label: List Workflows
nodes.title:
List Workflows
List GitHub Actions workflows in a repository. Requires owner + repo. Returns workflow_ids.
Provides: workflow_id
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: List Workflow Runs
nodes.title:
List Workflow Runs
List recent workflow run history. Requires owner + repo. Returns run_ids.
Provides: run_id
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Trigger Workflow
nodes.title:
Trigger Workflow
Manually trigger a workflow dispatch event. Requires owner + repo + workflow_id.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, workflow_id
Requires (user): ref

user_inputs.description: Branch or tag to run workflow on
Workflow dispatch inputs as key-value pairs

nodes.label: Cancel Workflow Run
nodes.title:
Cancel Workflow Run
Cancels a workflow run in a GitHub repository if it is in a cancellable state (e.g., 'in_progress' or 'queued'). Returns 202 Accepted on success.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, run_id

nodes.label: Approve Fork Workflow
nodes.title:
Approve Fork Workflow
Approves a workflow run from a forked repository's pull request; call this when such a run requires manual approval due to workflow configuration.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, run_id

nodes.label: List Deployments
nodes.title:
List Deployments
Lists deployments for a specified repository; repository must exist.
Provides: deployment_id
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Create Deployment
nodes.title:
Create Deployment
Create a deployment targeting a ref (branch, tag, SHA). Requires owner + repo + ref.
Provides: deployment_id
Requires (tool): owner, repo, branch_name

user_inputs.description: Auto-merge default branch into requested ref

nodes.label: Update Deployment Status
nodes.title:
Update Deployment Status
Update the operational state of a deployment. Requires owner + repo + deployment_id.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, deployment_id
Requires (user): state

user_inputs.description: Deployment state: error | failure | inactive | in_progress | queued | pending | success

nodes.label: List Releases
nodes.title:
List Releases
Retrieves a list of all releases (published, draft, and prerelease) for a specified repository.
Provides: release_id
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Get Release
nodes.title:
Get Release
Retrieve release details. Requires owner + repo + release_id.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, release_id

nodes.label: Create Release
nodes.title:
Create Release
Create a new release (with tag). Requires owner + repo.
Provides: release_id
Requires (tool): owner, repo
Requires (user): tag_name

user_inputs.description: Git tag for this release (e.g. v1.2.0)

nodes.label: List My Orgs
nodes.title:
List My Orgs
List organizations for the authenticated user. Returns org names.
Provides: org

nodes.label: List Teams
nodes.title:
List Teams
Lists teams for a specified GitHub organization.
Provides: team_slug
Requires (tool): org

nodes.label: Create Team
nodes.title:
Create Team
Create a new team in an organization. Requires org. Returns team_slug.
Provides: team_slug
Requires (tool): org
Requires (user): name

nodes.label: List Org Members
nodes.title:
List Org Members
List members of an organization. Requires org. Returns usernames.
Provides: username
Requires (tool): org

nodes.label: Update Team Membership
nodes.title:
Update Team Membership
Adds a GitHub user to a team or updates their role (member or maintainer), inviting them to the organization if not already a member; idempotent, returning current details if no change is made.
Requires (tool): org, team_slug, username

nodes.label: List Collaborators
nodes.title:
List Collaborators
List collaborators for a repository. Requires owner + repo. Returns usernames.
Provides: username
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Add Collaborator
nodes.title:
Add Collaborator
Adds a GitHub user as a repository collaborator, or updates their permission if already a collaborator; `permission` applies to organization-owned repositories (personal ones default to 'push' and ignore this field), and an invitation may be created or permissions updated directly.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, username

user_inputs.description: Access level: pull | triage | push | maintain | admin

nodes.label: List Repo Invitations
nodes.title:
List Repo Invitations
Retrieves all pending (unaccepted or undeclined) collaboration invitations for a specified GitHub repository.
Provides: invitation_id
Requires (tool): owner, repo

nodes.label: Accept Invitation
nodes.title:
Accept Invitation
Accepts a PENDING repository invitation that has been issued to the authenticated user.
Requires (tool): invitation_id

nodes.label: Create Check Run
nodes.title:
Create Check Run
Creates a new check run for a specific commit in a repository, used by external services to report status, detailed feedback, annotations, and images directly within the GitHub UI. NOTE: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication - OAuth tokens and personal access tokens cannot create check runs.
Requires (tool): owner, repo, commit_sha
Requires (user): name

user_inputs.description: Check run display name (e.g. Unit Tests)
Conclusion when completed: success | failure | neutral | cancelled

edges.label: body
edges.title:
User provides body (string) → Reply to Thread
Reply message text

edges.label: subject
edges.title:
User provides subject (string) → Send Email
Email subject line
Example: Meeting notes

edges.label: body
edges.title:
User provides body (string) → Send Email
Email message body

edges.label: name
edges.title:
User provides name (string) → Create Label
Label display name
Example: Urgent

edges.title:

Person's name or partial email to search for
Example: John Smith

edges.label: summary
edges.title:

Calendar title
Example: Work

edges.label: summary
edges.title:
User provides summary (string) → Create Event
Event title
Example: Team standup

Start datetime ISO 8601 (e.g. 2024-06-01T10:00:00Z)

edges.label: end_time
edges.title:
User provides end_time (string) → Create Event
End datetime ISO 8601

edges.label: role
edges.title:

Access role: reader | writer | owner

Scope type: user | group | domain

edges.title:

File name
Example: report.pdf

Comment text

edges.title:

Permission role: reader | writer | commenter | owner

edges.title:

Principal type: user | group | domain | anyone

edges.title:

Spreadsheet title
Example: Budget 2024

edges.label: range
edges.title:

A1 notation range to clear
Example: A1:Z100

edges.label: values
edges.title:
User provides values (array) → Write Cells
2D array of cell values to write

edges.label: range
edges.title:
User provides range (string) → Write Cells
A1 notation range (e.g. Sheet1!A1:C5)

edges.label: operation
edges.title:
User provides operation (string) → Aggregate Column
Operation: sum | average | count | min | max

edges.label: target_column

Column letter to aggregate (e.g. B)

edges.label: sheet_name

Task list name
Example: Sprint 5

Task title
Example: Review pull request

Array of task objects [{title, notes?, due?}]

Album name
Example: Vacation 2024

Date range(s): [{startDate, endDate}] e.g. [{startDate:'7daysAgo',endDate:'today'}]

Metric names e.g. ['sessions','users','pageviews']

Date range(s) for the pivot report

Pivot definitions [{fieldNames, limit}]

Tree entries [{path, mode, type, sha or content}]
